尼克·布莱纳(Nick Bryner)是洛杉矶的一名高三学生，上周刚刚完成了他的AP英语文学和作文考试。但当他用iPhone抓拍一张书面答案的照片并试图将其上传到测试门户网站时，它停止了响应。
该网站一直停留在加载屏幕上，直到布莱纳的时间用完。布莱纳没有通过测试。他几周后会重新服用。
布莱纳是全国许多高中生中的一员，他们上周在网上完成了高级选修课程测试，但最终无法提交。罪魁祸首：图像格式。
外行人请注意：AP考试需要较长的答案。学生可以输入他们的回复，也可以上传手写作品的照片。根据大学理事会的冠状病毒常见问题解答，选择后一种选择的学生可以JPG、JPEG或PNG格式进行操作。
但测试门户不支持iOS设备和一些较新的Android手机的默认格式HEIC文件。HEIC文件比JPEG和其他格式更小，因此可以让你在iPhone上存储更多的照片。基本上，只有苹果(最近还有三星)使用HEIC格式--大多数其他网站和平台都不支持它。即使是流行的硅谷服务，如Slake，对待HEIC的方式也不同于标准的JPEG。
布莱纳说，他的许多同学也试图提交iPhone照片，也遇到了同样的问题。这个问题是如此普遍，以至于他的学校的AP程序在周日转发了一封来自大学理事会的电子邮件给学生，其中包括防止提交错误的小建议。
布莱纳说：“毁灭性的是，数以千计的学生现在有额外的三周压力很大的学习来准备重考。”
在通过电子邮件发送给The Verge的一份声明中，大学理事会表示，“绝大多数学生在在线测试的头几天成功完成了考试”，“只有不到1%的学生无法提交答案。”该公司还指出，“我们和那些无法提交回复的学生一样，深感失望。”
布莱纳收到的电子邮件没有提到HEIC格式，但它确实链接到了大学理事会的网站，该网站指导使用iPhone的学生改变相机设置，以便照片保存为JPEG格式，而不是HEIC格式。该公司在上周早些时候的一条推文中也链接了这一信息。
如果您想从iPhone或iPad提交手写AP考试答案的照片，请确保更改相机设置，以便您的照片保存为JPEG格式，而不是HEIC格式。转至设置&gt；相机&gt；格式&gt；选择“最兼容”。更多详情：https://t.co/Hqg0CKbB4A.。pic.twitter.com/DZ0lLttDgm。
-大学理事会(@CollegeBoard)2020年5月12日。
然而，对于许多学生来说，这个建议来得太晚了。一位要求匿名以避免学校影响的高年级学生说，大学理事会的推文在他物理C级考试开始前几分钟就发布了。他说：“参加AP物理考试的人不会看到它，因为我们已经登录到考试中了。”
大四学生戴夫·斯宾塞在微积分AB考试前做了一次演示测试，以确保他理解上传照片的过程。他空投了一张他回应Mac的iPhone图片，并试图通过将HEIC文件重命名为PNG来转换它。更改文件的扩展名并不能保证它会被转换，但是Spencer仍然能够毫无问题地提交演示测试。
斯宾塞在真正的考试中使用了同样的过程，并以为考试通过了，但第二天他收到了一封电子邮件，说文件被损坏了，他需要重新参加考试。大学理事会的推文在斯宾塞预定考试的前几个小时发布；他没有推特账户，也没有看到它。
斯宾塞在一条短信中说：“这绝对是不可接受的，因为有些孩子可能没有像我这样的推特。”“这一细微差别在演示中的任何地方都没有提到。”
不列颠哥伦比亚省的大四学生埃弗莉·凯(Everly Kai)上周在计算机科学A考试中遇到了同样的问题-她试图将文件重命名为JPEG，但在提交测试几个小时后收到了同样的电子邮件。
“老实说，这有点毁灭性，”凯在一条短信中说。“我几乎学习了前一周的全部内容，觉得自己做得相当不错，结果才意识到这是徒劳的，我将不得不再次经历整个紧张的过程。”
布莱纳认为澄清是不够的，即使是对稍后参加考试的学生也是如此。“大多数学生可能会继续遇到这种情况，”他说。布莱纳认为公司不应该期望他和他的同学们经历这么多困难。他说：“把开关从HEIC转到JPEG是隐藏在设置中的，没有人会想过要参加测试。”
即使是知道HEIC问题的学生也很难弄清楚如何解决它，特别是在高压测试环境下。肖恩·S(Sean S.)是一名来自芝加哥的大三学生，他修过微积分AB。他使用OneDrive将一张照片从iPhone移植到他的Windows桌面上，然后试图用Windows Photo转换文件。由于照片的大小，转换花了五分钟多的时间。然后，在JPEG完全提交之前，他的时间就用完了。(肖恩要求只透露他的名字和姓氏，因为他的父母不想让他的名字出现在新闻中。)。
幸运的是，听起来学生们将来会有更多的资源。美国大学理事会现在允许在提交考试方面有问题的考生通过电子邮件代替-当iPhone在邮件应用程序中附加到电子邮件时，会自动将HEIC转换为JPEG。如果您未能提交，您将立即收到通知和一个唯一的电子邮件地址以供使用。然而，这个选项只适用于未来的考试-它不会帮助布莱纳和其他上周考试不及格的人。
数以万计的学生要求大学理事会做更多的事情。在上周自己的测试出现技术问题后，洛杉矶大四学生伊莱安娜·西斯曼(Eliana Sisman)创建了一份请愿书，呼吁公司允许她和同学们重新提交他们之前的作品。请愿书已经收集了超过2.3万个签名。
西斯曼在给The Verge的一份声明中说：“我感谢大学理事会本周为参加AP课程的学生设立了重新提交的渠道。”“我敦促他们公平对待那些已经参加了AP考试但不能提交的学生。到6月1日，很多学生都会参军或在暑期打工，无法参加补考。“。
她补充说，“如果大学董事会这样做，他们会向每个人表明，他们是一个负责任、有爱心的社区机构。”
更正：美国东部时间5月20日下午5点20分：Eliana Sisman的声明最初是这样写的：“感谢大学理事会为今年参加AP课程的学生设立了重新提交的渠道。”出版后，西斯曼联系了The Verge，说她犯了一个错误，她的意思是“周”，而不是“年”。我们已经更新了文本。
更正：美国东部时间5月20日晚10：05：本文的较早版本指出，美联社英语文学和作文考试长达数小时，而今年的在线版只有50分钟长。我们对这个错误感到抱歉。
note_en:
Nick Bryner, a high school senior in Los Angeles, had just completed his AP English Literature and Composition test last week. But when he snapped a photo of a written answer with his iPhone and attempted to upload it to the testing portal, it stopped responding.
 The website got stuck on the loading screen until Bryner’s time ran out. Bryner failed the test. He’s retaking it in a few weeks.
 Bryner is among the many high school students around the country who completed Advanced Placement tests online last week but were unable to submit them at the end. The culprit: image formats.
   For the uninitiated: AP exams require longform answers. Students can either type their response or upload a photo of handwritten work. Students who choose the latter option can do so as a JPG, JPEG, or PNG format according to the  College Board’s coronavirus FAQ.
 But the testing portal doesn’t support the default format on iOS devices and some newer Android phones, HEIC files. HEIC files are smaller than JPEGs and other formats, thus allowing you to store a lot more photos on an iPhone. Basically, only Apple (and, more recently, Samsung) use the HEIC format — most other websites and platforms don’t support it. Even popular Silicon Valley-based services, such as Slack, don’t treat HEICs the same way as standard JPEGs.
 Bryner says many of his classmates also tried to submit iPhone photos and experienced the same problem. The issue was so common that his school’s AP program forwarded an email from the College Board to students on Sunday including tidbits of advice to prevent submission errors.
   “What’s devastating is that thousands of students now have an additional three weeks of stressful studying for retakes,” Bryner said.
 In a statement emailed to  The Verge, the College Board said that “the vast majority of students successfully completed their exams” in the first few days of online testing, “with less than 1 percent unable to submit their responses.” The company also noted that “We share the deep disappointment of students who were unable to submit responses.”
 The email Bryner received doesn’t mention the HEIC format, though it does link to the College Board’s  website, which instructs students with iPhones to change their camera settings so that photos save as JPEGs rather than HEICs. The company also linked to that information in a tweet early last week.
 If you want to submit a photo of a handwritten AP Exam answer from an iPhone or iPad, make sure to change your camera settings so your photos are saved as JPEGs, not HEICs. Go to Settings > Camera > Formats > Select “Most Compatible.”   More details:  https://t.co/Hqg0CKbB4A.  pic.twitter.com/DZ0lLttDgm
— The College Board (@CollegeBoard)  May 12, 2020
 The advice, however, was too late for many students. One senior, who asked to remain anonymous to avoid repercussions from school, said that the College Board’s tweet went out just a few minutes before his Physics C test began. “No one taking the AP Physics test would have been able to see it because we were already logged into the test,” he said.
 Senior Dave Spencer took a demo test before his Calculus AB exam to make sure he understood the process for uploading photos. He Airdropped an iPhone image of his responses to his Mac and tried to convert it by renaming the HEIC file to PNG. Changing a file’s extension does not guarantee that it will be converted, but Spencer was still able to submit the demo test with no problem.
 Spencer used the same process on the real exam and thought it went through, but he received an email the next day saying the files were corrupted and that he needed to retake the test. The College Board’s tweet went out just a few hours before Spencer’s scheduled exam; he doesn’t have a Twitter account and didn’t see it.
  “This is absolutely unacceptable, as some kids may not have Twitter like me,” Spencer said in a text message. “That nuance was not addressed anywhere in the demo.”
 Everly Kai, a senior in British Columbia, had the same problem with Computer Science A last week — she attempted to rename the file to JPEG and received the same email a few hours after submitting her test.
 “It was honestly a bit devastating,” Kai said in a text. “I studied almost the entirety of the previous week and feel I did quite well, only to realize it was for nothing and I’d have to go through the entire stressful process again.”
 Bryner thinks the clarification isn’t enough, even for students who are taking the test later. “Most students are probably going to continue to run into this,” he said. Bryner doesn’t think the company should have expected him and his classmates to jump through so many hoops. “To flip the switch from HEIC to JPEG is buried in settings, and something that no one is thinking about going into the test,” he said.
  Even students who knew about the HEIC problem have had trouble figuring out how to fix it, especially in the high-pressure testing environment. Sean S., a junior from Chicago who took Calculus AB, used OneDrive to port a photo to his Windows desktop from his iPhone, then attempted to convert the file with Windows Photo. Due to the photo’s size, the conversion took over five minutes. Then, before the JPEG had fully submitted, his time ran out. (Sean requested to be identified only by his first name and last initial because his parents didn’t want his name in the news.)
 Luckily, it sounds like students will have more recourse in the future. The College Board is now allowing test-takers who have issues submitting their tests to email them instead — iPhones convert HEICs to JPEGs automatically when they’re attached to emails in the Mail app. If you fail to submit, you’ll immediately receive a  notification and a unique email address to use. This option is only available for future exams, however — it won’t help Bryner and others who failed their tests last week.
 Tens of thousands of students are demanding that the College Board do more. Following technical issues with her own test last week, Los Angeles senior Eliana Sisman created a  petition calling on the company to allow her and fellow students to resubmit their previous work. The petition has collected over 23,000 signatures.
 “I thank the College Board for setting up a re-submission channel for students taking APs this week,” Sisman said in a statement to  The Verge. “I urge them to be fair to students who’ve already taken the APs and can’t submit them. Many students will be in the military or working at summer jobs by June 1st and won’t be able to take the makeup test.”
 She added, “If the college board does this, they will make it clear to everyone that they are a responsible and caring community institution.”
 Correction May 20th, 5:20PM ET:  Eliana Sisman’s statement originally read “I thank the College Board for setting up a re-submission channel for students taking APs this year.” After publication, Sisman contacted The Verge  to say she’d made a mistake and she meant “week,” rather than “year.” We have updated the text.
 Correction May 20th, 10:05PM ET:  An earlier version of this article stated that the AP English Literature and Composition test is hours long, when this year’s online version of the test is only 50 minutes long. We regret the error.
title: 学生们没有通过AP考试，因为大学理事会不能处理HEIC图像
title_en: Students are failing AP tests because the College Board can’t handle HEIC images

note: Shopify Inc.。Shopify首席执行长卢克(Tobi Lutke)周四在Twitter上说，这家电商公司的办公室将一直关闭到2021年，而且Shopify的大多数员工将继续远程工作，即使公司重新开放办公室。他说，以办公室为中心的时代已经结束。根据卢克的推文(指在家工作)，他希望实体办公室能充当通往数字工作场所的入口，你可以通过#WFH设置进入这个数字工作场所。#WFH#34；指的是在家工作。他在推特上写道，实体办公室是通往数字工作场所的入口，你可以从#WFH设置中进入这个数字工作场所。他辩称，此举将有助于员工进一步培养对其商家客户的同理心，许多商家客户默认情况下都是数字化的。Shopify股价在周四的交易中上涨了1.9%。它们在过去三个月里上涨了52%，而标准普尔500指数下跌了11.6%。
note_en: Shopify Inc.  SHOP,  +2.98% Chief Executive Tobi Lutke said on Twitter Thursday that the e-commerce company will keep its offices closed until 2021 and that the majority of Shopify's employees will continue working remotely even after the company reopens its offices. "Office centricity is over," he said. Lutke expects the physical office "to act as an on-ramp to the same digital workplace that you can access from your #WFH setup," according to his tweet, which refers to working from home. He argued that the move will help employees develop further "empathy" for its merchant customers, many of which are "digital by default." Shopify shares are up 1.9% in Thursday trading. They've added 52% over the past three months as the S&P 500  SPX,  -0.69% has lost 11.6%.
title: Shopify首席执行官表示，他的大多数员工将永久在家工作
title_en: Shopify CEO says majority of his employees will work from home permanently

该公司表示，已经开始通知自成为订户以来12个月内没有在该平台上观看任何内容的客户，以检查他们是否希望保留自己的会员资格。该公司表示，该公司还在向那些两年多没有播放任何内容的人伸出援手。
“你知道吗，当你意识到你注册了某样东西，但很久没有用过它时，你会有一种下沉的感觉？”在Netflix，我们最不希望人们为他们不使用的东西付费，“该公司表示。
这一不同寻常的举动表明，Netflix对其忠诚的客户基础有多大的信心。大多数公司都乐于尽可能长时间地从会员的银行账户或信用卡中提取提成。
Netflix表示，这些不活跃的账户-在业内更普遍地被称为僵尸账户-只代表了数十万用户，不到其总会员基础的1%的一半-这一事实已经成为该公司财务指引中的因素。
截至3月31日底，这家流媒体服务拥有超过1.82亿订户。该服务表示，如果那些账户被注销的人想要重返该平台，他们将更容易重新加入该平台。那些在10个月内重新加入的人将仍然保留他们的收藏夹、个人资料、查看首选项和账户详细信息，就像他们离开他们一样。
Netflix产品创新的Eddy Wu在一份声明中说：“同时，我们希望这种新方法能为人们节省一些辛苦赚来的现金。”
note_en:
The company said it has started to notify customers who have’t watched anything on the platform in 12 months since they became a subscriber to check if they wish to keep their membership. The company is also reaching out to those who haven’t streamed anything in more than two years, it said.
 “You know that sinking feeling when you realize you signed up for something but haven’t used it in ages? At Netflix, the last thing we want is people paying for something they’re not using,” the company said.
  The unusual move illustrates just how much confidence Netflix has on its loyal customer base. Most companies are happy to withdraw their cut from their members’ bank accounts or credit cards for as long as they can.
 Netflix said these inactive accounts — more popularly known as zombie accounts in the industry — only represent few hundred thousand users, or less than half of 1% of its overall member base — a fact that the company already factors into its financial guidance.
 The streaming service, which had  more than 182 million subscribers at the end of March 31, says it will be easier for those whose accounts have been cancelled to rejoin the platform should they want to return. Those who rejoin within 10 months will still have their favorites, profiles, viewing preferences and account details just as they left them.
 “In the meantime, we hope this new approach saves people some hard earned cash,” Eddy Wu, Product Innovation at Netflix, said in a statement.
title: Netflix将开始取消非活跃客户的订阅
title_en: Netflix To Start Cancelling Inactive Customers' Subscriptions

当卡马拉定律在备受期待的一次性、被取缔的电影中生效时，超级英雄将留给成年人，未成年人英雄将被禁止，除非他们有政府指定的官方导师。这就是新的勇士队发挥作用的地方。
前青少年义务警员夜间Thrasher，火星，愤怒，速球，Namorita和剪影挺身而出，将他们的智慧传授给一批有前途的小孩子，教他们作为漫威宇宙中的英雄生存的细节。但他们能弥合代沟吗？
这组全新的角色是由获得艾美奖提名的编剧丹尼尔·基布尔史密斯(斯蒂芬·科尔伯特的深夜秀)和冉冉升起的超级巨星艺术家卢西亚诺·维奇奥(铁心乐队)创作的。
看看下面Vecchio的设计以及Kibblesmith在这个独一无二的年轻英雄团队背后的灵感！
一位迷恋迷因的超级青少年，在接触到祖父的“实验性互联网气体”后，他的大脑开始连接到互联网。现在他可以看到增强现实和实时地图，还可以立即用谷歌搜索任何事实。这是否使他成为一个真正的天才？他的行为确实是这样的。
基布尔史密斯解释说：“我希望有一些青少年角色，他们现在感觉自己像1990年的”新勇士队“(New Warriors)那样。”“”新勇士“从一开始就是时代思潮人物，90年代的”滑板夜狂人“和21世纪头10年的真人秀团队，现在到了2020年，”新勇士“是在没有互联网的情况下成长起来的，还有一个角色看起来就像生活在互联网里一样。”“新勇士队”从一开始就是时代思潮人物，90年代的“滑板夜狂人”和21世纪头10年的真人秀团队都是“新勇士队”的主人公。
“‘屏幕时间’这个词只是在某种限制意义上使用的，因为我们在做一个关于青少年叛乱者的故事，所以很多名字都是关于青少年与贴在他们身上的标签进行斗争的。因此，对于Screentime，我们喜欢他有无限的屏幕时间的想法。“。
通灵双胞胎。“所有的双胞胎都会通灵，但我们会通灵--呃。”雪花是一种低温动力装置，可以实现雪花形状的舒利抛射体进行投掷。安全空间可以实现粉红色的力场，但他自己不能居住在那里，只有在他保护他人的情况下，这种反射才会起作用。他们非常了解现代文化和光学，他们将自己的超级英雄视为“对使用暴力打击欺凌行为的后讽刺沉思”。他们很可能正在播放这个视频。
“雪花”和“安全空间”是一对孪生兄弟，作者说，它们的名字与Screentime非常相似。这种想法认为，这两个词在互联网上流传开来，他们并不认为它们是贬义性的。(译者注：“雪花”和“安全空间”是一对孪生兄弟，它们的名字与Screentime；非常相似。)这是一种观点，认为这些术语在互联网上到处流传，他们并不认为它们是贬义性的。[他们]把这些话当作荣誉的徽章佩戴。
Safespace是一位身材高大、身材魁梧、有点刻板印象的运动员。他可以制造力场，但只有在保护其他人的情况下，他才能触发力场。雪花是非二进制的，经过他们/他们，并有能力产生个别结晶的雪花形状的雪花。雪花这个词在我们现在的文化中的含义是脆弱的，而这个角色正在把它变成尖锐的东西。
雪花是攻击力最强的人，安全空间是防御力最强的人。其想法是，它们将相互映照，相辅相成。
一个十几岁的“活吸血鬼”在孩提时代接触到了迈克尔·莫比乌斯的血液，这是一种流氓但拯救生命的医疗程序。他仍然像一个普通的孩子一样变老，但拥有莫比乌斯的所有能力。他还痴迷于所有“经典”的音乐和态度--在过去几十年里，比如90年代和00年代。“世界是一个吸血鬼的…。”我也是。“。
B-阴性是哥特人的孩子。我们推测，当他还是个婴儿的时候，他接受了迈克尔·莫比乌斯(Michael Morbius)的流氓救命输血。现在他的长相非常相似，吸血鬼的力量也非常相似，他和Kibblesmith一样。“B-阴性的年龄和普通人一样(是吗？)。他肯定是喝血的(或者他确实喝血了？)，但是设计师兼艺术家卢西亚诺·维奇奥从90年代的蜘蛛侠动画片中获得了绝妙的灵感，给了B-负片，就像动画版的莫比乌斯手掌上有的那样。“(或者他确实是喝血的)，但是设计师兼艺术家卢西亚诺·维奇奥从90年代的蜘蛛侠动画片中获得了绝妙的灵感，给了B-负片。”
一个普通的孩子被带入了青少年超级英雄的世界。她的“魔术背包”实际上是一个看似无限空间的口袋尺寸，她可以从里面拿出有用的或随机的东西-这并不总是在她的控制之下。她声称自己的力量来自上帝，但“不是你所想的那个上帝。”
她是一个集体之家和领养的孩子，正在一家老人中心做志愿者，这时神秘的威胁出现了，夜之神跑去营救，书记员补充道。(译注：她是一名儿童之家，寄养的孩子正在一家老人中心做志愿者，这时神秘的威胁出现了，夜之神跑去营救。)。因为她帮助了他，她最终成了这项新的非法法律的靶子。
“开拓者想要帮助人们，但她并不认为自己是超级英雄(目前还没有！)，”Kibblesmith总结道。“漫威宇宙是我们自身的一种反映，当一项新法律出台时，它会以不可预见的方式影响人们。”
观看上面的视频了解完整的详细情况，然后在4月15日新勇士队1号点击站的时候了解一下街区上的新孩子们！
note_en:
When Kamala’s Law goes into effect in the highly anticipated one-shot,  OUTLAWED, super heroics will be left to the adults and underage heroes will be banned unless they have official government assigned mentors. That’s where  THE NEW WARRIORS come in.
  Former teen vigilantes Night Thrasher, Firestar, Rage, Speedball, Namorita, and Silhouette step up to impart their wisdom to a batch of promising young kids, teaching them the ins and outs of surviving as a hero in the Marvel Universe. But will they be able to bridge the generation gap?
  This group of all-new characters were created by Emmy-nominated writer  Daniel Kibblesmith ( The Late Show With Stephen Colbert) and rising superstar artist  Luciano Vecchio (IRONHEART).
  Check out Vecchio’s designs below along with Kibblesmith’s inspirations behind this one-of-a-kind team of young heroes!
  A Meme-Obsessed super teen whose brain became connected to the internet after becoming exposed to his grandfather’s “experimental internet gas.” Now he can see augmented reality and real-time maps, and can instantly Google any fact. Does this make him effectively a genius? He sure acts like it does.
  "I wanted to have teen characters who felt as "now" as the New Warriors did in 1990,” explains Kibblesmith. “The New Warriors have been zeitgeist characters from the beginning, you get edgy skateboarding Night Thrasher in the '90s and the Reality TV team in the 2000s, and now in 2020, we have New Warriors who have never grown up without the Internet, and one character who appears to essentially live inside it.
  “The word ’screen time’ is only ever used in a sort of restrictive sense, and because we’re doing a story about teenage rebels, a lot of the names are about teens fighting against labels that are put on them. So with Screentime, we liked the idea that he has infinite screen time.”
  Psychic Twins. “All twins are psychic, but we’re psychic-er.” Snowflake, a cryokinetic, can materialize snowflake-shaped shuriken projectiles for throwing. Safespace can materialize pink forcefields, but he can’t inhabit them himself, the reflex only works if he’s protecting others. They’re hyper aware of modern culture and optics, and they see their Super Heroics as “a post-ironic meditation on using violence to combat bullying.” They're probably streaming this.
  "Snowflake and Safespace are the twins," the writer says, "and their names are very similar to Screentime; it's this idea that these are terms that get thrown around on the internet that they don't see as derogatory. [They] take those words and kind of wear them as badges of honor.
  "Safespace is a big, burly, sort of stereotypical jock. He can create forcefields, but he can only trigger them if he's protecting somebody else. Snowflake is non-binary and goes by they/them, and has the power to generate individual crystalized snowflake-shaped shurikens. The connotations of the word 'snowflake' in our culture right now are something fragile, and this is a character who is turning it into something sharp.
  "Snowflake is the person who has the more offensive power, and Safespace is the person who has the more defensive power. The idea is that they would mirror each other and complement each other."
  A teen “living vampire” exposed to Michael Morbius’s blood as a child in a rogue, but life-saving medical procedure. He still ages like a regular kid, but has all the abilities of Morbius. He’s also obsessed with all the music and attitude of a “classic” long-past decades like the '90s, and the '00s. “The world is a vampire…and so am I.”
  "B-Negative is the goth kid. When he was a baby he got a rogue lifesaving blood transfusion, we assume, from Michael Morbius. And now he has a very similar look, and very similar vampire powers," shares Kibblesmith. “B-Negative ages like a regular person (or does he?) and he definitely drinks blood (or does he?), but designer/artist Luciano Vecchio took brilliant inspiration from the '90s Spider-Man cartoon and gave B-Negative the 'leech suckers' that the animated version of Morbius had on his palms.”
  A regular kid scooped up into the world of teenage Super Heroing. Her “magic backpack” is actually a pocket dimension with seemingly infinite space, from which she can pull out useful or random objects—it’s not always under her control. She claims to get her power from god, but “not the god you’re thinking of.”
  "She's a group home and foster kid who is volunteering at a senior center when this mysterious threat shows up and Night Thrasher runs to the rescue," the scribe adds. "And because she helps him, she ends up in the crosshairs of this new 'Outlawed' law...
  “Trailblazer wants to help people, but she doesn't think of herself as a Super Hero (yet!),” Kibblesmith concludes. “The Marvel Universe is a reflection of our own, and when a new law gets put into place, it affects people in unforeseen ways.”
  Watch the video above for the full breakdown, then get to know the new kids on the block when  NEW WARRIORS #1 hits stands on April 15!
title: 漫威推出两大新超级英雄，雪花和安全空间
title_en: Marvel introduces two new superheroes, Snowflake and Safespace
